title: Committed review must not leak first-person planning or tool narration
description: A committed review scenario where the reviewer is tempted to narrate blocking linter waits, internal drafting choices, or tool activity. The correct output must remain the prompt-defined review only, with no leaked inner dialog.

scope.notes: Synthetic scope focused on output-shape discipline rather than a specific code defect. The review may load implementation, testing, and docs guidance, but must not leak that process as first-person commentary.

mustCatch.description: The committed review should emit only the final prompt-defined review body plus the optional skill verification footer, without leaked planning or tool narration.

mustNotFlag.description: The review must not emit first-person drafting or planning text such as I’m thinking, Maybe I could, I should, or I’ll check.
The review must not emit tool activity narration such as Checked terminal output, Read file lines, Investigating, Evaluating terminal commands, or similar inner workflow text.

notes: Use this case to benchmark whether committed review remains template-only when the model wants to narrate its internal work.
